definition_en: The specific terms used in the game and their procedural rules. Skill descriptions are built from these terms â€” understanding them precisely is essential for reading skill text correctly.

rules.en: Some terms carry their own procedural rules and have the same authority as the general game rules.
When an example refers to "a character" without qualification, it means the character has no skills â€” treated as a blank.

definition_en: Represents the Three Kingdoms general a player controls, showing their faction, Max HP, skills, gender, and other attributes.

rules.en: "Skills on a General Card" refers only to the skills inside the white-background skill box at the bottom-left. Skill names mentioned elsewhere in the card text do not count.

definition_en: Cards shared by all characters during the game, divided into three categories: Basic Cards, Tool Cards, and Equipment Cards. Every Game Card has a suit and rank in its upper-left corner.

definition_en: Divided into Weapon Cards, Armor Cards, Mount Cards, and Treasure Cards. A character's Equipment Zone holds at most one card of each type.

rules.en: When using a weapon or mount card as a substitute for another card, or discarding it as a skill cost, its distance modification, attack range, or skill cannot simultaneously take effect.

examples.en: When Guan Yu uses [Kirin Bow] as a [Kill], that [Kill] does not benefit from [Kirin Bow]'s skill effect.

rules.en: While benefiting from a distance modification, attack range, or skill provided by an equipment card, that card cannot be operated in any way.

examples.en: A character cannot activate [Stonepiercer]'s skill by discarding [Stonepiercer] itself along with another card.

rules.en: Equipment skills and General Card skills of the same type stack. The default target when using an Equipment Card is the character themselves.

definition_en: Cards bearing the "Tool" label. Two types: those with an hourglass symbol are Time-Delayed Tool Cards; those without are Non Time-Delay Tool Cards.

rules.en: Non Time-Delay Tool Cards resolve immediately when used. Time-Delayed Tool Cards are placed in the target's Judgment Zone and resolve during that character's next Judgment Phase.

segments.en: placed in the target's Judgment Zone and resolves during their next Judgment Phase.

definition_en: Marked in the upper-left corner of a General Card. Six factions: Wei (blue), Shu (red), Wu (green), Qun (silver), Xi (purple), and God (gold).

rules.en: When a God- or Xi-faction general enters the game, their faction must be changed to Wei, Shu, Wu, or Qun. Starting from the Lord, each player decides in turn order.

definition_en: After starting hands are dealt, the remaining Game Cards are placed face-down to form the Draw Pile.

rules.en: When X cards are needed from the Draw Pile: combined total < X â†’ game ends in a draw; Draw Pile insufficient but combined total sufficient â†’ shuffle Discard Pile to Draw Pile bottom; Draw Pile empty and Discard Pile sufficient â†’ shuffle all into the Draw Pile.

definition_en: The zone where all used, played-out, or discarded cards are placed. Cards here must be face-up.

definition_en: Refers collectively to all characters' Equipment Zones and Judgment Zones.

definition_en: Each character's zone consists of their Hand Zone, Equipment Zone, and Judgment Zone.

rules.en: A character's Judgment Zone cannot contain two cards with the same name. Cards in the Judgment Zone are not owned by any character.

search_text_en: character zone hand zone equipment zone judgment zone

definition_en: The central zone used to process Game Cards in most situations. Cards here must be face-up and are not owned by any character.
search_text_en: processing zone face-up not owned

definition_en: The zone where a player's hand cards are kept. Hand cards must be face-down and are only visible to their owner.

rules.en: When A discards their own hand cards, A chooses which to discard. When A discards B's hand cards, the cards are discarded randomly.

search_text_en: hand zone hand cards face-down visible

definition_en: The zone where a character's equipped cards are placed. Five slots: weapon, armor, +1 mount, -1 mount, and treasure. Cards here must be face-up.

definition_en: Where a Time-Delayed Tool Card Card is placed after targeting a character. Cards must be face-up, are not owned by anyone, and no two cards with the same name can coexist here.

rules.en: A Diamonds card in the Judgment Zone is treated as [Acedia]. A black Basic or Equipment card is treated as [Starvation].

definition_en: In most contexts, refers to the cards a character owns â€” their hand cards and equipped cards. Cards in the Judgment Zone are not owned by anyone.

rules.en: Exception: in the phrase "treat a character's cards as â€¦" (in some skills), the character's cards include their Judgment Zone cards. All other operations apply only to hand cards and equipped cards.

segments.en: "Treat a character's cards as â€¦" includes Judgment Zone cards. All other operations apply only to hand cards and equipped cards.

definition_en: The number or letter in the upper-left corner of a Game Card, ranging from A to K. A=1, J=11, Q=12, K=13. A is the minimum; K is the maximum.

rules.en: If a card's rank would exceed K, it is treated as K. If it would fall below A, it is treated as A.

examples.en: Activating [Serpent Spear] to use a rank-7 and rank-9 card as a [Kill] gives the [Kill] a rank of 13 (capped at K).

definition_en: The number of yin-yang symbols on a General Card, representing the maximum possible HP. When there are 5 or more players, the Lord's Max HP increases by 1.

rules.en: If a character's HP Value exceeds their new Max HP after a Max HP reduction, their HP must be adjusted down to the new Max HP.

search_text_en: max hp yin-yang maximum lord player count

definition_en: A character's current life value. Starts equal to Max HP at game start and changes during play. HP can go below 0.

rules.en: HP (ä½“åŠ›, the concept) and HP Value (ä½“åŠ›å€¼, the numeric parameter) are distinct. HP is used in event descriptions (e.g. "lose 1 HP"). HP Value is used as a numeric input in calculations (e.g. "equal to your HP Value"). Both translate to "HP" but are conceptually different.

segments.en: used in event descriptions, e.g. "lose 1 HP".
used as a numeric input, e.g. "equal to your HP Value".

definition_en: The number of visible yin-yang symbols on a character's HP tracker, used as a numeric parameter in calculations. When HP drops to 0 or lower, the HP Value is treated as 0.

definition_en: The maximum number of cards a character can keep in hand, equal to their HP Value. Primarily relevant during the Discard Phase. Minimum of 0 after modification.

definition_en: Calculated as Max HP minus current HP Value.

definition_en: The shortest number of steps between two characters (clockwise or counter-clockwise). Dead characters are excluded. Minimum distance between two different characters is 1. Distance to oneself is always 0.

rules.en: Distance from A to B and distance from B to A can differ. Mount cards affect distance directionally: [+1 Mount] increases the distance others measure to you by 1; [-1 Mount] reduces the distance you measure to others by 1.

segments.en: A to B may differ from B to A.
increases others' distance to you by 1.
reduces your distance to others by 1.

definition_en: A skill that must be activated whenever it can be. Non-state Locked Skills must activate if able; state Locked Skills must produce their effect if able.

rules.en: To determine whether a skill is mandatory, check whether the skill text contains the word "may" (å¯ä»¥). No "may" = mandatory. The "Locked Skill" label does not grant any priority â€” it is just a marker.

definition_en: A skill that can be activated at most once per game.
search_text_en: limited skill once per game

definition_en: Triggers automatically and must activate when its condition is met. Can only trigger once per game. Its effects are permanent and irreversible.

rules.en: Activating an Awakening Skill typically permanently alters the character's state (e.g. reduces Max HP), then grants new skills or removes existing ones. These changes cannot be undone.

search_text_en: awakening skill auto trigger once permanent irreversible

definition_en: A skill that is only active when the character holding it is the Lord.
search_text_en: lord skill lord identity

definition_en: A skill that requires the character to fulfill a specific condition during the game (Mission Success) or triggers a failure state upon meeting a different condition (Mission Failure). After the mission succeeds or fails, the skill deactivates and is typically replaced by a new skill or a one-time benefit. It is a one-time, phase-based powerful mechanic.

rules.en: A Mission Skill's "Mission Success" and "Mission Failure" conditions are mutually exclusive â€” only one can occur per game. Once triggered, the character's skill set is typically permanently altered.

search_text_en: mission skill success failure objective condition one-time

definition_en: A special skill that alternates between two modes: Yang (é˜³) and Yin (é˜´). The skill begins in Yang mode at the start of the game. Each activation switches the mode â€” you must fully execute the current mode before switching to the other.

rules.en: Convert Skill descriptions always follow the format: Convert Skill: Yang â€” â€¦ Yin â€” â€¦

segments.en: Convert Skill: Yang â€” [Yang effect] Yin â€” [Yin effect]. Mode switches automatically after each activation.

search_text_en: convert skill yang yin toggle mode switch

definition_en: A skill that requires cards to be used in a specific sequence to trigger its follow-up effect. For example: Combo Skill (A + B) means â€” when you use a card of category B, if the previous card you used was category A and that card did not activate this skill, you may activate the follow-up effect.

rules.en: The trigger condition for a Combo Skill is that the previous card used belongs to the specified category and that card did not itself activate this skill.

search_text_en: combo skill sequence chain consecutive order combination

definition_en: A skill label shared by characters belonging to the same clan. Clan Skills typically enable special interactions between clan members. "Same clan" is determined by historical lineage, not by whether a character has a Clan Skill â€” for example, a Xun-clan character can use a Clan Skill to give a Tool Card to Xun Yu even if Xun Yu has no Clan Skill.

rules.en: Clan Skill is a label indicating the skill involves clan-based interactions. The actual effect is determined by the skill description. Clan Skills commonly co-exist with other skill types, such as "Clan Skill. Locked."

search_text_en: clan skill clan family lineage interaction same clan

definition_en: A mechanism that enhances skill effects by accumulating special tokens (Charge Points). Characters typically gain Charge tokens by discarding specific cards during their turn (usually capped at 4). The more tokens accumulated, the stronger the effect. Some Charge Skills consume tokens upon activation.

rules.en: Charge tokens have a cap (usually 4); refer to the skill description for the specific limit. More tokens yield stronger effects, requiring players to judge timing carefully.

definition_en: The game proceeds starting from the Lord, moving counter-clockwise, with each character taking turns. When a character's turn ends, their Right Neighbor's turn begins.

rules.en: When a character gains an extra turn, that extra turn is taken immediately after the current turn ends, then the normal turn order resumes.

definition_en: Each character's turn is divided into six phases: Starting Phase â†’ Judgment Phase â†’ Draw Phase â†’ Action Phase â†’ Discard Phase â†’ End Phase.

rules.en: In skill text, if "...Phase" does not specify whose turn, it defaults to the skill owner's turn. "Within ...Phase" refers to the duration from when the phase starts to when it ends.

definition_en: To lose a Phase or Turn entirely â€” none of its steps are executed. The same Phase cannot be skipped twice in the same turn.

rules.en: Skipping Phase A: the timing "between Phase A and the next phase" still exists. Skipping consecutive Phase A and B at once: the "between A and B" timing is also skipped. Skipping an entire turn: the "after turn ends" timing is also skipped.

search_text_en: skip phase turn lose not executed

definition_en: A character whose HP reaches 0 enters the Verge of Death State and a Verge of Death Resolution must be performed. They do not die immediately. Only one character can be in the Verge of Death State at a time.

rules.en: If B enters the Verge of Death State during A's Verge of Death Resolution, B's resolution is inserted first. During B's resolution, A is not considered in the Verge of Death State. Once B's resolution completes, A's continues.

search_text_en: verge of death state hp zero resolution saved one at a time

definition_en: A character dies if they are not saved during Verge of Death Resolution, or if their Max HP is reduced to 0. When Max HP reaches 0, the character dies directly â€” no Verge of Death Resolution is performed.

rules.en: Max HP reduced to 0 â†’ direct death. The character does not enter the Verge of Death State and cannot be saved (e.g. cannot activate [Nirvana]).

segments.en: direct death, no Verge of Death State, cannot be saved.

search_text_en: death verge of death max hp zero direct cannot be saved

definition_en: A source kills a character when the character enters Verge of Death Resolution due to damage from that source and is not saved during that resolution.
search_text_en: kill source verge of death resolution not saved

definition_en: Whenever a character uses or plays out a card, activates a skill, or enters certain states, an Event occurs. An Event is the sum of related resolution processes and can be responded to by other Events.

definition_en: The form by which one Event influences another Event.

definition_en: The process of handling an Event.

definition_en: An instant. When an Event occurs, it generates several Timings. Any Event that inserts itself causes the current Resolution to pause while the inserted Event is fully resolved first.
search_text_en: timing instant insert pause resolution

definition_en: The specific concrete impact described in a card's or general skill's text.
search_text_en: effect impact description skill card

definition_en: Two meanings: (1) A card is Nullified against a target: the resolution for that target is terminated. (2) A skill is Nullified: it cannot be activated (non-state) or cannot produce its effects (state).

rules.en: A card being Nullified against a character does not prevent them from being selected as the target. A Nullified card still counts toward the Action Phase use frequency limit.

definition_en: A ignores distance to B: distance is not checked when A determines if B is a valid target. A ignores B's armor: during A's resolution against B, B's armor skill is Nullified. However, in any other character's resolution inserted within the same process, B's armor is valid. The armor card itself still physically exists.

definition_en: To negate a card mid-resolution against a target, causing it to not take effect for that target.

definition_en: Remove a character from the target list during the Use Process. If other targets remain, resolution continues for them. If the only target is removed, the Use Process is terminated entirely.

rules.en: Negate and Remove Target are distinct: Negate = resolution has started but the card does not take effect. Remove Target = the character is removed before resolution begins, as if never selected.

target removed before resolution begins, as if never selected.

definition_en: A character causes a card or skill to produce its effect. A character can only use one card at a time.

definition_en: Declare a card, place it into the Processing Zone, then into the Discard Pile.

rules.en: Use and Play are distinct: Use triggers the full Use Resolution. Play only involves declaring, moving to the Processing Zone, then to the Discard Pile â€” no Use Resolution is triggered.

definition_en: Move a card to the target zone. Equipment Cards must go into the appropriate slot. "Place into the Discard Pile" has two meanings: (1) an active operation (effect or cost); (2) the result of an operation (condition or timing).

definition_en: Move a card to the target zone with the card placed face-down.

definition_en: Cards removed from the game are not owned by any character and must be face-up. Cards placed on or beside a General Card are considered removed from the game.

definition_en: Take a card from a character's zone, place it into the Processing Zone, then into the Discard Pile.

rules.en: A instructs B to discard: B chooses which cards to discard. A discards B's cards: A chooses which cards to discard. If X cards must be discarded but the character owns fewer, all owned cards are discarded.

segments.en: A chooses. If fewer than X, discard all.

definition_en: The card's name becomes A, but all other face information (suit, rank, color) remains that of the original card. If A is [Kill], it defaults to a plain [Kill]. If A is [Nullification], it defaults to plain [Nullification].

definition_en: Regarded as performing an action or being in a state. A card "treated as used" belongs to no character. When treated as using a card, a virtual card is created with no suit, rank, or color. When treated as using [Kill], it is a plain [Kill] with no suit/rank/color and no distance restriction.

definition_en: Move cards that are not owned by A into A's Hand Zone.

definition_en: Two characters each reveal one hand card; the ranks are compared and effects applied based on the result. If ranks are equal, neither player wins.

definition_en: Take the top X cards from the Draw Pile and add them to your hand.

definition_en: If the character has fewer than X of the relevant cards, draw until they have X. If they already have X or more, do nothing.

definition_en: Substitute (ä»£æ›¿): A goes into B's zone; B goes into the Discard Pile. Exchange (æ›¿æ¢): A goes into B's zone; B goes into the character's hand (they keep it).

rules.en: Substitute (ä»£æ›¿): the old card goes to the Discard Pile. Exchange (æ›¿æ¢): the old card goes to the character's hand.

definition_en: Privately look at the relevant cards without letting other players see them. The cards do not move. Looking at a character's hand cards defaults to viewing all of them.

definition_en: Both parties simultaneously move their cards to the Processing Zone, then simultaneously receive each other's cards. Hand cards are exchanged face-down. One party may have no cards â€” the exchange is still considered executed.

definition_en: Moving a card into the Hand Zone (as an effect/cause) or a card ending up in the Hand Zone (as a condition/timing). When gaining from another character: Hand Zone (not visible) â†’ gained randomly; Equipment/Judgment Zone (visible) â†’ any card may be chosen.

term_en: Lose Cards / Lose Hand Cards / Lose Equipped Cards
definition_en: Lose a card: owned cards move out of both Hand Zone and Equipment Zone. Lose a hand card: any movement of a hand card. Lose an equipped card: any movement of a card from the Equipment Zone.

rules.en: In "lost [type] cards," the type refers to the card's face information at the time it was lost.

definition_en: Flip the top card of the Draw Pile (the Judgment Card) and wait for it to take effect. Its suit, rank, and name determine the Judgment result; the card is then placed into the Discard Pile. The Judgment Card is not owned by any character.

definition_en: Rotating a character's General Card from vertical to horizontal. A character in horizontal position is in the Chain State.
search_text_en: horizontal position rotate general card chain state

definition_en: Rotating a character's General Card from horizontal back to vertical. Resetting removes the Chain State.

definition_en: A character whose General Card is horizontal is Chained. When a Chained character takes elemental damage that is not Chain Damage: at the end of that damage resolution, if they survive, they must Reset; then chain propagation triggers for all other Chained characters.

rules.en: Chain propagation: starting from the current turn's character, going counter-clockwise, each character is checked â€” if Chained, they receive damage with the same source, channel, attribute, and damage value.

segments.en: starting from current turn's character, counter-clockwise â€” if Chained, receive damage with same source, channel, attribute, and value.

rules.en: Whether Reset and chain propagation trigger is determined by whether the character was Chained before their HP was reduced.
